assessments.status_rationale: Today’s news bullets contain no indication that NASA has launched Artemis II; the deadline (2026-04-05) has not yet passed.
No item in today’s bullets suggests Adobe has filed an 8-K or equivalent release naming a next CEO; deadline is 2026-06-30.
No bullet indicates Meta has publicly launched a new flagship, new-generation Llama major-version model; deadline is 2026-06-30.
Today’s bullets reference Fed-related legal/political developments but provide no clear evidence about FOMC rate-cut decisions through the 2026-06-17 meeting.

Several stories discuss Middle East deployments and Strait of Hormuz concerns, but none clearly state a publicly confirmed escorted transit of a commercial oil tanker through the Strait of Hormuz; deadline is 2026-04-30.
No bullet reports an OFAC/U.S. Treasury extension or expansion of written authorization for transactions involving Russian-origin crude already loaded 'at sea'; deadline is 2026-05-15.
No bullet mentions USTR publishing a Federal Register notice proposing Section 301 remedies tied to March 2026 forced-labor probes meeting the specified scope/thresholds; deadline is 2026-06-30.
No bullet references Fitch publishing a report/press release that U.S. private credit default rates reached ≥10% (as defined by Fitch) for a trailing-12-month period ending by 2026-06-30.
No bullet indicates FDA granted Breakthrough Device Designation to a blood-based Alzheimer’s early-detection test; deadline is 2026-12-31.
No bullet indicates a new Phase 1/2 interventional trial registration on ClinicalTrials.gov for a narrow-spectrum, microbiome-sparing C. diff antibiotic; deadline is 2026-12-31.
